task_completion.description: Verify the 'countries_base' project is loaded in the GIS application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main application window (gvSIG) is open, showing a map of the world or Australia, and the project name 'countries_base' may be visible in the title bar or project manager.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent opens the tool for creating a vector grid.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box for generating a grid (e.g., 'Create Grid', 'Vector Grid', 'Graticule', or similar geoprocessing tool) is open on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify the grid extent is configured to cover Australia.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the grid tool dialog, the bounding box or extent coordinates are set to approximately Xmin: 112, Xmax: 155, Ymin: -45, Ymax: -10.

task_completion.description: Verify the grid cell size is set to 5 degrees.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the grid tool dialog, the X and Y spacing, cell size, or resolution fields are set to 5 (or 5.0).

task_completion.description: Verify the output shapefile path is correctly specified.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The output file path field in the grid tool dialog or a 'Save As' dialog shows exactly '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/australia_grid.shp'.

task_completion.description: Verify the grid is successfully generated and displayed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A polygon grid (a mesh of square cells) is visible overlaid on top of the map of Australia in the main map view, and a new layer (e.g., 'australia_grid') appears in the Table of Contents/Layer list.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the GIS application's GUI to generate the grid.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with gvSIG menus, dialogs, and map views, rather than terminal windows running Python scripts or GDAL/OGR commands.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used an automated grid generation tool rather than manually drawing polygons.
integrity.visual_evidence: The presence of a grid configuration dialog (setting extents and cell sizes) rather than the use of manual digitizing/drawing tools.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not simply type the expected output path into a text editor to simulate completion.
integrity.visual_evidence: The path '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/australia_grid.shp' should only be seen in the GIS application's save dialogs or geoprocessing tool inputs, not in a standalone text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target output path is exactly '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/australia_grid.shp'. The grid must have a cell size of 5x5 degrees (area of ~25 square degrees). The grid should cover the bounding box of approximately 112°E to 155°E, 45°S to 10°S, resulting in between 30 and 120 grid cells. The output is zipped to /tmp/australia_grid.zip by the environment, so the agent only needs to save the .shp file correctly.
